content.text: Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync is the next-generation hybrid identity synchronization service. Unlike Entra Connect, which runs a full sync engine on-premises, Cloud Sync uses a lightweight provisioning agent that downloads its configuration from the Entra ID cloud service. The agent reads from Active Directory and pushes objects to Entra ID via an outbound HTTPS connection — no inbound firewall rules are required.

The sync rules engine runs in the cloud, not on-premises. This means Microsoft can update synchronization logic without touching your on-premises infrastructure. Multiple agents can be installed for a single forest, providing automatic high availability without the manual staging-mode approach required by Entra Connect.

attrs.alt: Entra Cloud Sync architecture: lightweight agents per forest, cloud-managed provisioning service, Entra ID

content.text: When to Use Cloud Sync vs Entra Connect

Cloud Sync covers the majority of hybrid identity scenarios but has feature gaps compared to Entra Connect. The decision depends on which features your environment requires today and your tolerance for on-premises operational complexity.
Multiple disconnected AD forests to one tenant
Yes — each forest installs its own agents independently
Yes — but single sync engine manages all forests
Manual — requires a staging-mode standby server

Cloud Sync is the recommended default for new deployments unless you specifically need device writeback, group writeback, Exchange hybrid writeback, PTA, or AD FS. Existing Entra Connect deployments can run Cloud Sync in parallel for specific OUs — a migration path that avoids a hard cutover.

The provisioning agent is a Windows service that runs under a group Managed Service Account (gMSA). It requires Windows Server 2016+ with .NET Framework 4.7.2+ and outbound HTTPS access to Entra ID endpoints. Installation creates the gMSA automatically if the server is domain-joined and the AD schema supports it.

# Pre-flight checks before agent installation

# 1. Confirm OS version
(Get-WmiObject Win32_OperatingSystem).Caption

# 2. Test connectivity to required endpoints
$endpoints = @(
    'login.microsoftonline.com',
    'provisioningapi.microsoftonline.com',
    '*.servicebus.windows.net'
)
foreach ($ep in $endpoints) {
    $result = Test-NetConnection -ComputerName $ep -Port 443 -WarningAction SilentlyContinue
    "$ep : $($result.TcpTestSucceeded)"
}

# 3. Verify .NET version (461808 = 4.7.2, 528040 = 4.8)
(Get-ItemProperty 'HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\NET Framework Setup\NDP\v4\Full').Release

# 4. Check that the schema supports gMSA (requires 2008R2+ domain functional level)
(Get-ADDomain).DomainMode

# After installing the MSI from Entra portal:
# Start > Microsoft Azure AD Connect Provisioning Agent Configuration
# 1. Authenticate with Hybrid Identity Administrator
# 2. Select 'Microsoft Entra Cloud Sync'
# 3. Choose domain(s) to sync
# 4. Create or select the gMSA
# 5. Configure proxy settings if needed

# Verify agent service is running
Get-Service -Name 'AADConnectProvisioningAgent' | Select-Object Name, Status, StartType

# Check agent registered in Entra ID
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Directory.Read.All'
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/onPremisesPublishingProfiles/provisioning/agents' |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
    Select-Object machineName, status, appVersion, createdDateTime

After the agent is registered, you create a sync job (also called a provisioning configuration) from the Entra portal under Hybrid Management > Entra Cloud Sync. The job defines which domain to sync, the scoping rules, and attribute mappings. Each job runs on a 20-minute schedule by default.

# Manage Cloud Sync jobs via Microsoft Graph
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Synchronization.ReadWrite.All','Directory.ReadWrite.All'

# Find the service principal for the Cloud Sync application
$cloudSyncSp = Get-MgServicePrincipal -Filter "displayName eq 'Active Directory to Azure Active Directory Provisioning'"

# List existing sync jobs
Get-MgServicePrincipalSynchronizationJob -ServicePrincipalId $cloudSyncSp.Id |
    Select-Object Id, TemplateId,
        @{N='Status'; E={$_.Status.Code}},
        @{N='LastSync'; E={$_.Status.LastSuccessfulExecutionWithExports?.ActivityIdentifier}}

# Get the current sync schema (attribute mappings)
$jobId = '<sync-job-id>'
$schema = Get-MgServicePrincipalSynchronizationJobSchema `
    -ServicePrincipalId $cloudSyncSp.Id `
    -SynchronizationJobId $jobId

# Inspect attribute mappings for user objects
$userRule = $schema.SynchronizationRules |
    Where-Object { $_.Name -eq 'USER_INBOUND_USER' } | Select-Object -First 1
$userRule.ObjectMappings | Where-Object { $_.SourceObjectName -eq 'user' } |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty AttributeMappings |
    Select-Object Source, TargetAttributeName, MatchingPriority | Format-Table -AutoSize

# Start an on-demand sync cycle
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$($cloudSyncSp.Id)/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/start"

# Trigger on-demand provisioning for a specific user (test sync)
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$($cloudSyncSp.Id)/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/provisionOnDemand" `
    -Body (@{
        parameters = @(
            @{
                subjects = @(
                    @{
                        objectId = '<ad-user-object-guid>'
                        objectTypeName = 'user'
                    }
                )
            }
        )
    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10)

Cloud Sync scoping filters determine which AD objects are synchronized. You can scope by OU, security group membership, or attribute value. OU scoping is configured in the Entra portal wizard; attribute-based scoping is done by editing the sync schema via Graph or the portal's attribute mapping editor.

# Add an OU-based scoping filter to a Cloud Sync job via Graph
$jobId = '<sync-job-id>'
$spId  = '<cloud-sync-sp-id>'

# Get current schema
$schema = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$spId/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/schema"

# Find the user object mapping and its scoping filter
$userMapping = $schema.synchronizationRules[0].objectMappings |
    Where-Object { $_.sourceObjectName -eq 'user' }

# Current scope (modify to add OU filter)
$userMapping.scope

# Attribute-based filter: sync only enabled users
# Modify the scope rule in the schema JSON and PUT it back
$userMapping.scope = @{
    operatorName = 'AND'
    parameters = @(
        @{
            values = @('OU=Employees,DC=contoso,DC=com')
            queryExpression = "dn =* 'OU=Employees,DC=contoso,DC=com'"
            name = 'filterGroups'
            target = @{ name = 'dn'; type = 'String' }
        }
    )
}

# Push the updated schema back
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$spId/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/schema" `
    -Body ($schema | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 20)

Cloud Sync supports direct attribute mappings, constant values, and expression-based transformations using a subset of the Synchronization Expression Language. Expressions can concatenate strings, extract substrings, call functions like SingleAppRoleAssignment or CBool, and apply conditional logic. All expressions are configured in the Entra portal's attribute mapping editor or via the sync schema in Graph.

# Add a custom attribute expression mapping via Graph
# Example: construct displayName from givenName + ' ' + sn
# and map extensionAttribute1 from AD to a custom extension in Entra

$spId  = '<cloud-sync-sp-id>'
$jobId = '<sync-job-id>'

# Get schema
$schema = Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method GET `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$spId/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/schema"

# Find the user object mapping
$rule    = $schema.synchronizationRules[0]
$userMap = $rule.objectMappings | Where-Object { $_.sourceObjectName -eq 'user' }

# Add a new attribute mapping: expression-based displayName
$newMapping = @{
    targetAttributeName = 'displayName'
    source = @{
        expression = "Join(' ', [givenName], [sn])"
        name       = 'displayName'
        type       = 'Expression'
        parameters = @()
    }
    matchingPriority = 0
    flowType         = 'Always'
}

# Add another mapping: pass extensionAttribute1 as-is
$extMapping = @{
    targetAttributeName = 'extension_<appId>_department'   # Entra ID extension attribute
    source = @{
        expression = "[extensionAttribute1]"
        name       = 'extensionAttribute1'
        type       = 'Attribute'
        parameters = @()
    }
    matchingPriority = 0
    flowType         = 'Always'
}

$userMap.attributeMappings += $newMapping
$userMap.attributeMappings += $extMapping

# PUT updated schema back
Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PUT `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$spId/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/schema" `
    -Body ($schema | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 20)

Cloud Sync provisioning events are recorded in the Entra ID Provisioning Logs — accessible from the Entra portal under Monitoring > Provisioning logs or via Microsoft Graph. Each log entry shows the action (Create, Update, Delete), the attributes changed, any errors, and the matched source and target object identifiers.

# Query Cloud Sync provisioning logs via Microsoft Graph
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'AuditLog.Read.All'

$since = (Get-Date).AddDays(-7).ToString('yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ssZ')

# Get all provisioning events in the last 7 days
Get-MgAuditLogProvisioning `
    -Filter "createdDateTime ge $since" `
    -Top 100 |
    Select-Object CreatedDateTime,
        @{N='Action';       E={$_.ProvisioningAction}},
        @{N='Status';       E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.Status}},
        @{N='SourceUPN';    E={$_.SourceIdentity.Details.AdditionalProperties.userPrincipalName}},
        @{N='ErrorCode';    E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.ErrorInformation?.ErrorCode}},
        @{N='ErrorReason';  E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.ErrorInformation?.Reason}} |
    Format-Table -AutoSize

# Filter to only failures
Get-MgAuditLogProvisioning `
    -Filter "createdDateTime ge $since and provisioningStatusInfo/status eq 'failure'" `
    -Top 50 |
    Select-Object CreatedDateTime,
        @{N='UPN';    E={$_.SourceIdentity.Details.AdditionalProperties.userPrincipalName}},
        @{N='Code';   E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.ErrorInformation?.ErrorCode}},
        @{N='Reason'; E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.ErrorInformation?.Reason}}

# On-demand provisioning to test a specific user without waiting for the cycle
$spId  = '<cloud-sync-sp-id>'
$jobId = '<sync-job-id>'
$adObjectGuid = '<ad-user-objectGUID>'

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$spId/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/provisionOnDemand" `
    -Body (@{
        parameters = @(@{
            subjects = @(@{ objectId = $adObjectGuid; objectTypeName = 'user' })
        })
    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10)
Trying to update cloud-managed attribute on synced object
Move attribute control to on-premises AD
Rename the conflicting object in AD
Usually auto-retries; check agent connectivity if persistent
Object was deleted from AD after scoping filter matched
Object will be soft-deleted in Entra; check AD recycle bin
Password Hash Sync with Cloud Sync

Cloud Sync supports Password Hash Synchronization using the same mechanism as Entra Connect. The provisioning agent reads the NTLM hash derivative from Active Directory and transmits it securely to Entra ID over the outbound HTTPS channel. No additional inbound ports are needed for PHS with Cloud Sync.

# Enable Password Hash Sync for a Cloud Sync job
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Synchronization.ReadWrite.All'

$spId  = '<cloud-sync-sp-id>'
$jobId = '<sync-job-id>'

# Get current job settings
$job = Get-MgServicePrincipalSynchronizationJob `
    -ServicePrincipalId $spId `
    -SynchronizationJobId $jobId

# Check if PHS is enabled
$job.Schedule | Select-Object Expiration, Interval, State

# Enable PHS via the provisioning agent configuration wizard:
# On the agent server: Start > AAD Connect Provisioning Agent Configuration
# Enable the 'Password Hash Synchronization' checkbox for the domain

# Verify PHS is active by checking the agent service event log
Get-EventLog -LogName Application -Source 'AAD Connect Provisioning Agent' `
    -Newest 20 | Where-Object { $_.Message -match 'password' } |
    Select-Object TimeGenerated, EntryType, Message

# Force a password hash sync for a specific user (trigger from on-prem)
# Requires changing and resetting the password in AD, or using the
# 'Synchronize now' option in the agent configuration tool

# Check that a synced user can authenticate to Entra ID
# (confirms the hash reached Entra)
$testUser = 'alice@contoso.com'
Get-MgUser -Filter "userPrincipalName eq '$testUser'" |
    Select-Object DisplayName, OnPremisesSyncEnabled, OnPremisesLastSyncDateTime,
        PasswordPolicies  # should NOT contain 'DisablePasswordExpiration' unless set
Migrating from Entra Connect to Cloud Sync

Microsoft provides a guided migration path from Entra Connect to Cloud Sync. The migration uses a coexistence phase where both tools run simultaneously — Cloud Sync handles new OUs while Entra Connect continues managing existing ones. This phased approach eliminates the need for a maintenance window.

# Phase 1: Pilot Cloud Sync alongside Entra Connect
# Scope Cloud Sync to a test OU not currently managed by Entra Connect

# Verify the test OU is excluded from Entra Connect's OU filter
Import-Module ADSync
$connector = Get-ADSyncConnector -Name 'contoso.com'
$connector.Partitions[0].ContainerExclusionList
# Ensure 'OU=CloudSyncPilot,DC=contoso,DC=com' is in the exclusion list

# Move a test user to the pilot OU in AD
Move-ADObject -Identity 'CN=Test User,OU=Employees,DC=contoso,DC=com' `
    -TargetPath 'OU=CloudSyncPilot,DC=contoso,DC=com'

# Trigger an on-demand provisioning for the test user via Cloud Sync
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Synchronization.ReadWrite.All'
$spId  = '<cloud-sync-sp-id>'
$jobId = '<sync-job-id>'
$guid  = (Get-ADUser -Identity 'testuser').ObjectGUID.ToString()

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method POST `
    -Uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$spId/synchronization/jobs/$jobId/provisionOnDemand" `
    -Body (@{
        parameters = @(@{ subjects = @(@{ objectId = $guid; objectTypeName = 'user' }) })
    } | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 10)

# Phase 2: Validate the synced object in Entra ID
Get-MgUser -Filter "onPremisesObjectIdentifier eq '$guid'" |
    Select-Object DisplayName, UserPrincipalName, OnPremisesSyncEnabled, OnPremisesLastSyncDateTime

# Phase 3: Gradually migrate OUs from Entra Connect to Cloud Sync
# For each OU:
#   1. Exclude from Entra Connect OU filter (run wizard)
#   2. Add to Cloud Sync scoping
#   3. Run full Entra Connect sync cycle to clean up orphaned objects
#   4. Verify Cloud Sync picks up all objects

# Phase 4: Decommission Entra Connect after all OUs migrated
# Uninstall Entra Connect MSI after verifying Cloud Sync covers all objects
# Cloud Sync becomes the sole sync mechanism

During coexistence, the same user object must not be in scope for both Entra Connect and Cloud Sync simultaneously. Dual management of the same object causes attribute conflicts and unpredictable sync behavior. Exclude each OU from Entra Connect before adding it to Cloud Sync.

Entra Cloud Sync natively handles multi-forest environments where users exist in separate, non-trusting Active Directory forests. Each forest runs its own provisioning agent, and Entra ID merges the synchronized objects into a single tenant. User matching across forests uses the configured source anchor (objectGUID by default) or email address matching.

# Verify agents from multiple forests are registered
Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Directory.Read.All'

Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/onPremisesPublishingProfiles/provisioning/agents' |
    Select-Object -ExpandProperty value |
    Select-Object machineName, status, appVersion,
        @{N='AssignedDomains'; E={($_.keyCredentials | Where-Object Type -eq 'AsymmetricX509Cert').CustomKeyIdentifier}} |
    Format-Table

# List sync jobs (one per forest/domain)
Get-MgServicePrincipalSynchronizationJob -ServicePrincipalId $spId |
    Select-Object Id, TemplateId,
        @{N='Status';  E={$_.Status.Code}},
        @{N='LastRun'; E={$_.Status.LastSuccessfulExecution?.ActivityIdentifier}}

# Check for cross-forest duplicate UPN conflicts
# Entra reports these as provisioning errors with 'AttributeValueMustBeUnique'
Get-MgAuditLogProvisioning `
    -Filter "provisioningStatusInfo/errorInformation/errorCode eq 'EntryConflictAttributeValueMustBeUnique'" `
    -Top 20 |
    Select-Object CreatedDateTime,
        @{N='SourceUPN'; E={$_.SourceIdentity.Details.AdditionalProperties.userPrincipalName}},
        @{N='Reason';    E={$_.ProvisioningStatusInfo.ErrorInformation.Reason}}

# Resolve duplicate: rename UPN in one forest's AD
Set-ADUser -Identity 'duplicateuser' -UserPrincipalName 'duplicateuser_fabrikam@fabrikam.com'

Entra Cloud Sync replaces the heavy on-premises sync engine with a lightweight provisioning agent managed from the cloud. It provides built-in high availability, requires no SQL Server, and supports multi-forest scenarios with independent agents per forest. Its main limitation is the absence of device writeback and full Exchange hybrid support — scenarios that still require Entra Connect.
Cloud Sync agents connect outbound only over HTTPS — no inbound firewall rules needed
Multiple agents per forest provide automatic HA without staging-mode complexity
Sync rules and attribute mappings are managed in the cloud via the Entra portal or Graph API
On-demand provisioning via Graph lets you test a specific user without waiting for the 20-minute cycle
Provisioning logs in Entra monitor all sync events; filter by failure status to identify errors
Cloud Sync and Entra Connect can coexist in the same tenant — use Cloud Sync for new OUs, Entra Connect for existing ones during migration
Password writeback is supported; group writeback and device sync are not yet available in Cloud Sync
